name: Sintra, Pena Palace & Quinta da Regaleira Day Trip from Lisbon
tagline: Portugal's most magical palaces — a fairy-tale hilltop village just 40 minutes from Lisbon
overview: Sintra is one of Europe's most extraordinary day trips — a UNESCO World Heritage hilltop village where Portuguese royalty built their most fantastical summer retreats. Pena Palace crowns the highest peak in a blaze of yellow and red, while the mysterious Quinta da Regaleira hides underground tunnels, an initiatic well, and secret grottos in its romantic gardens. Both are among Portugal's most visited monuments.
highlights: Guided tour of Pena Palace, Portugal's most iconic royal residence
Explore Quinta da Regaleira's mysterious gardens and initiatic well
Walk the historic centre of Sintra, a UNESCO World Heritage Site
Small group for a personal, unhurried experience

itinerary.description: Explore the extraordinary Romantic palace built for King Ferdinand II in the 1840s. Its vivid yellow and red turrets, drawbridge, and eclectic mix of Gothic, Moorish, and Manueline styles make it unlike any other palace in Europe. Panoramic views over Sintra and the Atlantic.

Discover one of Sintra's most enigmatic estates — a neo-Manueline palace surrounded by gardens full of secret tunnels, underground grottos, and the famous Initiatic Well, a spiral staircase descending 27 metres into the earth with Masonic and Templar symbolism.
Free time to explore the UNESCO-listed village centre. Browse the shops along Rua das Padarias, see the National Palace's distinctive twin chimneys, and sample the famous travesseiros pastry.

meeting_point: Lisbon city centre — confirmed at booking
tips: Sintra is extremely busy in summer — this tour's small group size (max 20) makes a real difference
Wear comfortable shoes: both Pena and Regaleira involve significant uphill walking
The initiatic well at Regaleira can be cold and dark — bring a light layer
Book at least a week ahead between May and September

faqs.question: Is Sintra far from Lisbon?
faqs.answer: Sintra is only 40km from central Lisbon — about 40 minutes by road. It's easily the most accessible great palace day trip from any European capital.

faqs.question: Is Pena Palace the only castle in Sintra?
faqs.answer: No — Sintra has at least five palaces and fortresses. Pena Palace and Quinta da Regaleira are the highlights on this tour; the Moorish Castle and National Palace are also worth visiting on a second trip.

hero_image.alt: Pena Palace rising above the forest of Sintra in vivid yellow and red
